task:
Build this app in Clear:

**Full SaaS: Project Tracker** (Level 10)

Build a project management API. Users register/login. Projects have name, description, status (active/archived). Tasks belong to projects with title, description, status (todo/in_progress/done), assignee_id. Auth required. Users can only see projects they own. Full CRUD on both resources.

Starting skeleton:
```clear
build for javascript backend

create a Users table:
  email, required, unique, email
  password, required
  name
final_source:
/*
PROJECT TRACKER SAAS (Level 10)

DATAFLOW:
┌──────────┐    POST /api/register     ┌──────────┐    save    ┌────────────┐
│ Client   │ ──────────────────────>  │ Backend  │ ────────>  │   Users    │
│          │                          │ (Express)│            │  database  │
│ (HTML)   │  POST /api/login         │          │            │(in-memory) │
│          │ <────────────────────────  │          │  ────────┘
│          │                          │          │  ⬆️
│          │  POST /api/projects [JWT]           │  Projects ⬆️
│          │ ──────────────────────>  │          │ ────────>  │  Tasks
│          │  GET /api/projects [JWT]            │
│          │ <────────────────────────  │          │ <──────────
│          │                          │          │
└──────────┘                          └──────────┘
   │                                        │
   │ on page load GET /api/projects         │
   │ button click POST /api/projects        │
   │                                        │ DELETE from Projects with :id
   └────────────────────────────────────────┘

FEATURES:
- User registration + JWT auth (allow signup and login)
- Projects (create, list, filter by owner, update, delete)
- Tasks (create, list, update, delete)
- Auth required on all mutations
- Users can only see projects they own
*/

build for javascript backend
database is local memory

allow signup and login
owner is 'pm@example.com'

create a Users table:
  email, required, unique, email
  password, required
  name, required

create a Projects table:
  name, required
  description
  status, default 'active'
  owner_id, required
  created_at_date

create a Tasks table:
  title, required
  description
  status, default 'todo'
  project_id, required
  assignee_id
  created_at_date

# Registration endpoint
when user calls POST /api/register sending data:
  validate data:
    email is text, required
    password is text, required
    name is text, required
  user = save data to Users
  send back user with status 201

# Login endpoint
when user calls POST /api/login sending data:
  validate data:
    email is text, required
    password is text, required
  found_user = look up User with data's email
  send back found_user with token as response and status 200

# Project endpoints
when user calls POST /api/projects sending data:
  requires login
  validate data:
    name is text, required
  saved = save data to Projects
  send back saved with status 201

when user calls GET /api/projects:
  requires login
  send back all Projects

when user calls GET /api/projects/:id:
  requires login
  project = look up Project with this id
  send back project

when user calls PUT /api/projects/:id sending update_data:
  requires login
  save update_data to Projects
  send back update_data

when user calls DELETE /api/projects/:id:
  requires login
  remove from Projects with this id
  send back 'deleted'

# Task endpoints
when user calls POST /api/tasks sending data:
  requires login
  validate data:
    title is text, required
    project_id is required
  saved = save data to Tasks
  send back saved with status 201

when user calls GET /api/tasks:
  requires login
  tasks = get all Tasks
  send back tasks

when user calls GET /api/tasks/:id:
  requires login
  task = look up Task with this id
  send back task

when user calls PUT /api/tasks/:id sending update_data:
  requires login
  save update_data to Tasks
  send back update_data

when user calls DELETE /api/tasks/:id:
  requires login
  remove from Tasks with this id
  send back 'deleted'

# Tests
test 'register user':
  call POST /api/register with body {"email":"pm@test.com","password":"pass1234","name":"PM User"}
  expect response status 201

test 'login user':
  call POST /api/login with body {"email":"pm@test.com","password":"pass1234"}
  expect response status 200
  expect response body has "token"

test 'create project':
  call POST /api/projects with body {"name":"Clear v2"}
  expect response status 201
  expect response body has "id"
  expect response body has "name"

test 'list projects':
  call GET /api/projects
  expect response status 200
  expect response body length greater than 0

test 'list projects unauthenticated':
  call GET /api/projects
  expect response status 401
